lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. SCHOOL ASSEMBLY HALL – MORNING. Students fill the hall. Excited conversations echo through the room.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: The principal stands at the front.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: PRINCIPAL: We're happy to announce next week's science field trip!

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: The room erupts. Students immediately begin discussing buses, lunch plans, and who they're sitting with.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: LIAM, 15, sits quietly near the back.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: The screen freezes. Avery steps forward.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Most people think loneliness means being alone.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Doesn't it?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Sometimes.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: But sometimes it means standing in a crowd and still not knowing where you belong.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY. A large sign-up sheet hangs on the wall. Students crowd around it. Names are quickly filling the page.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Groups form almost instantly.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: JACK: We're taking the back seats.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ETHAN: Save me one.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: EMMA: Same.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Looks like everyone already knows where they're sitting.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Notice anyone who doesn't?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Liam is staring at the list. Not writing his name. Not leaving. Just waiting.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: What's he waiting for?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Maybe someone to ask first.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — difficulty initiating social interaction.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. CAFETERIA. Students talk about the upcoming trip. Trips they've taken before. Snacks they're bringing.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Liam sits nearby. Listening. Not participating.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Emma notices him.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: EMMA: You coming on the trip?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: LIAM: Yeah.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: EMMA: Cool.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: She smiles and returns to her friends.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: That seemed normal.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: It was.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Then why does he look uncomfortable?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Liam's thoughts: 'She was just being polite. They already have their group. Don't make it awkward.'

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: There it is.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — negative assumptions about social situations.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. DINING ROOM – EVENING. Dinner is quiet. Not hostile. Not warm either.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Dad scrolls through work emails. Mom checks her phone.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: LIAM: Trip's next week.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: MOM: Permission form signed?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: LIAM: Yeah.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: DAD: Good.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Silence. Liam hesitates.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: LIAM: I don't really know who I'm sitting with yet.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: For a moment, it feels like he's trying to say something more.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: DAD: You'll figure it out.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: MOM: Just focus on enjoying the trip.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Conversation moves on.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Wait.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: I think he was asking for reassurance.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: I think so too.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: And they didn't notice.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — lack of emotional support.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. SCHOOL BUS — TRIP DAY. Students board. Laughter. Music. Excitement.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Seats fill quickly. Groups settle in. Liam climbs aboard.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Looks around. There are empty seats. But every empty seat feels occupied.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Someone's going to invite him over.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Nobody does. Not because they dislike him. Because everyone assumes he'll sit somewhere.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Liam quietly chooses a seat by himself. The bus pulls away.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: That feels worse somehow.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because nobody did anything wrong.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — feeling isolated despite inclusion.

scenes.title: The Invitation
scenes.instructions: Liam drifts between groups at the museum. At an exhibit, Emma notices him. What does he do?

situations.context: Emma looks over: "Hey Liam. We're grabbing lunch after this. You should come."

choices.outcome: Emma smiles. He joins them for lunch. It's awkward at first, but he is there.
She waits for him to decide later, but he never follows up. The door was open, but he didn't walk through.
Emma nods and leaves him to explore alone. The isolation is confirmed, though self-inflicted.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. LIAM'S BEDROOM — EVENING. Trip finished. Phone in hand. New notifications.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: EMMA: glad u came today :)

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ETHAN: u should sit with us next time lol

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Well? They clearly like him.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Looks that way.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Liam smiles. A small one. He begins typing. 'thanks'

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Deletes. Types again. Deletes.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Locks the phone.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Even now?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Even now.

scenes.title: Chapter 1 — Liam Complete
scenes.summary: Liam wasn't excluded. He was invited, waved at, and told to sit with the group next time. But none of it reached him — because somewhere along the way he learned that his feelings weren't worth sharing, and that silence was safer than risk. Loneliness isn't always about being locked outside or unwanted. Sometimes it's about never feeling certain you're wanted, standing at the door, and never feeling able to walk through it.
keyLearnings: Difficulty initiating social interaction often stems from fear of awkwardness.
Negative assumptions ('They already have their group') override actual evidence of inclusion.
A quiet, distant family environment can teach children that emotional reassurance isn't available.
You can feel profoundly isolated even when included in a group or activity.
Loneliness isn't always about being unwanted. Sometimes it's about never feeling certain you're wanted.
